title: Agent Pulse
description: One daily evidence-backed decision brief from the coding-agent projects monitored by The Git Reporter.

scope.coverageStatement: V0.2 reports one dated decision brief from direct public source evidence and keeps git motion as a secondary measurement layer. Community feedback metrics are absent until a reproducible feedback collector ships.

inputs.dailyInsight: reported, date-owned Agent Pulse decision brief

editorialRead.headline: Morning Read — July 21, 2026
editorialRead.readHeadline: earendil-works/pi is the first source to inspect.
editorialRead.oneLineSummary: earendil-works/pi leads a 3-item attention queue backed by direct receipts; 24 watchlist sources are fresh, 0 need source-health attention, and movement still requires editorial judgment.
editorialRead.dek: Agent Pulse filtered 24 included projects down to 3 items worth finite newsroom attention. The queue protects attention; it does not declare winners.
bullets: earendil-works/pi — 30 commits today and 1 same-day mapped article, 17.6 above its current daily pace, with 30 hardening-subject points. Inspect the receipt before assigning a story.
openclaw/openclaw — 273 commits landed today. Keep it on watch, but require the next receipt to prove a reader consequence before promoting it.
browser-use/browser-use — No same-day commit after 9 commits in the seven-day window. The pause is notable enough to preserve, not enough to turn into a story.
editorialRead.watchNext: The next meaningful upgrade is feedback flow: issues opened and closed, review latency, stale backlog, and the share of work that starts outside the maintainer team.
editorialRead.caveat: Source-code motion and article coverage are collected. GitHub issue flow, PR review timing, community discussion, and private backlog signals are not.

dailyInsight.headline: An “always allow” permission must survive failure before it earns trust
dailyInsight.insight: OpenClaw fixed a plugin-binding path that could trust an “always allow” grant in memory before SQLite had durably recorded it. A failed write could therefore make the running agent more permissive than its saved policy.
dailyInsight.mechanism: The patch reverses the order: persist the approval first, publish it to the in-memory cache only after the write succeeds, then recompute from the current cache so concurrent approvals are not lost.
dailyInsight.whyItMatters: Durable agent permissions are security state. If a cache grants authority that storage rejected, the interface can report failure while the live process silently keeps the broader permission until restart.

actions.label: Audit the write order
actions.instruction: Find every durable approval path in your agent stack. Require the durable write to succeed before any in-memory policy cache begins authorizing from the grant.

actions.label: Inject a failed persist
actions.instruction: Force SQLITE_BUSY, disk-full, or read-only failure during an “always allow” decision. Repeat the same-scope action and verify that the agent prompts again instead of auto-approving from memory.

actions.label: Check before granting broadly
actions.instruction: If your deployed OpenClaw revision predates this commit, prefer one-time approval for plugin conversation bindings until you can test the failed-write path in your own environment.

receipts.title: fix(plugins): persist binding approval before trusting it in memory (#101906)
receipts.supports: Shows the cache-before-persist defect, the persist-first fix, and regression tests for failed writes and concurrent approvals.

confidence.basis: The source diff, maintainer explanation, and failure-injection regression test all support the mechanism-level claim.

dailyInsight.limitation: This is one source-level OpenClaw fix. The commit is not evidence that a particular release, package, or deployed instance already includes it, and it does not establish how common this write-order defect is across other agents.
dailyInsight.falsifier: This guidance should be revised if the commit is reverted, if another authorization layer proves the leaked cache entry could never approve a bind, or if the regression test no longer reproduces the failed-write boundary.
dailyInsight.watchNext: Watch for release inclusion and for other agent projects adding failure-injection tests around durable approvals, policy caches, or concurrent grants.
